**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Suzhou Zhongxin Power Polytron Technologies Inc. presents a classic case of an aging industrial entity transitioning into an asset-light corporate shell. Established in 1997 and structured as a non-listed joint-stock company with a paid-in capital of 20 Million RMB, it historically focused on power auxiliary machinery and environmental treatment equipment. However, its 2024 insured workforce has dwindled to exactly 1 employee. Furthermore, its registered address is an office room ('Room 2179, 2nd Floor') within the Suzhou Industrial Park, completely incongruous with heavy machinery manufacturing. The final, decisive indicator in its business scope is 'leasing of self-owned factory buildings'. This confirms that the entity has ceased active physical manufacturing, utilizing its single employee to manage the leasing of its legacy industrial real estate while likely outsourcing any remaining equipment sales or acting as a pure bidding shell.
